plan: Trim SPY to 20% to reduce concentration and meet the mandate.
Sell all SOXL and SPAL to exit leveraged ETFs and remove below-stop positions.
Increase QQQI to 25% for more income and lower volatility.
Increase QQQ to 20% to boost broad index exposure.
This plan prioritizes capital preservation and reduces overall portfolio risk.
conservative.read: This conservative plan targets a modest ~1.5% monthly return with a focus on capital preservation. It significantly reduces downside risk by eliminating leveraged ETFs and lowering concentration, but also limits potential upside.

plan: Trim SPY to 25% to reduce concentration and meet the mandate.
Sell all SOXL and SPAL to exit below-stop leveraged ETFs.
Increase CRWD to 15% to lean into a strong performer.
Buy NOW and ANET at 5% each, adding high-scoring, strong risk/reward setups.
This plan balances risk and growth, maintaining a diversified approach.
balanced.read: This balanced plan aims for ~2.5% monthly growth by taking calculated risks. It maintains a moderate risk profile by reducing overall leverage and diversifying into quality setups, while still seeking growth.

plan: Trim SPY to 25% to reduce concentration and meet the mandate.
Sell all SOXL and SPAL to exit below-stop positions.
Increase CRWD to 20% to press into a strong performer.
Buy TECL at 10% to add leveraged tech exposure.
Buy ARM at 10% as a high-scoring semiconductor setup.
This plan aggressively seeks growth by concentrating in strong names and using leverage.
aggressive.read: This aggressive plan targets ~4% monthly growth, but comes with significantly higher risk and potential for larger drawdowns. The use of leveraged ETFs amplifies both gains and losses, and daily decay/rebalancing can erode value quickly. Strict stop discipline is crucial.

plan: Trim SPY to 25% to reduce concentration and meet the mandate.
Sell all SOXL and SPAL to exit leveraged ETFs and below-stop positions.
Increase QQQI to 30% to maximize income focus.
Trim QQQ to 10% to reduce growth exposure and favor income.
Trim SMH to 5% to reduce high-beta semiconductor exposure.
This plan prioritizes steady monthly distributions and low drawdown.
income.read: This income-focused plan aims for ~1% monthly distributions, prioritizing stability over growth. While designed for steady income, distributions are variable and the NAV can still decline in market downturns. This is not a savings account.
